title: Phase Unwrapping (2D modulo-2pi inversion)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Ghiglia-Romero 1996 least-squares phase unwrap; Goldstein 1988 branch-cut; Pritt-Shipman 1994; Bioucas-Dias 2007 PUMA graph-cut; SnapHu (Chen-Zebker) InSAR; Spoorthi-Gorthi-Gorthi 2019 PhaseNet.

E.description: A true phase field phi(r) in R is wrapped into [-pi, pi] by modulo-2pi projection at acquisition (e.g., InSAR, MRI phase, holography). The task is to recover phi (2-pi ambiguity per pixel) from psi = wrap(phi) under noise and phase-discontinuity ambiguity.
E.forward_model: psi(r) = wrap(phi(r) + n(r)) = phi(r) + 2*pi*k(r);  k(r) in Z
E.world_state_x: Absolute phase phi in R^{H x W}, piecewise-continuous (up to true fringe jumps)
E.observation_y: Wrapped phase psi in [-pi, pi]^{H x W}
physical_parameters_theta: Discontinuity fraction (true jumps > pi per pixel)
Sampling/undersampling below Nyquist of local fringe

W.uniqueness: modulo constant 2pi; unique under Itoh's condition |grad phi| < pi per pixel
W.regime: Well-posed when Itoh condition holds everywhere; ill-posed around phase residues (where gradient circulation is non-zero). LS unwrap smooths but biases; branch-cut correct when cuts routed through low-coherence regions.

C.solver_class: LS (direct cosine transform), branch-cut (Goldstein), min-cost-flow (PUMA), quality-guided (Itoh path), deep (PhaseNet, DeepLab-unwrap)
C.error_bound: LS RMS error ~ sigma_phi / sqrt(coherence); branch-cut: exact when cut routing correct, else 2-pi discontinuities
C.complexity: LS: O(H*W*log(H*W)) via DCT; MCF: O((H*W)^{3/2}); deep: O(H*W*C*F) single forward
